reviews.title: Pretty good
reviews.text:
Pretty good smartwatch. I needed something to replace my Galaxy Active, but didn't want to spend $100+ on something with a horrible battery life. I bought this one and for a while I was disappointed. I had to charge the watch each night and the steps weren't as accurate. I changed the setting so that the screen doesn't turn on when you move your wrist and that has saved the battery big time. I'm on day 4 and only down to 50% which means I'm now able to track sleep as well. I still think the steps aren't as accurate because it almost matches the steps in my phone's health app, but I'm notorious for leaving my phone places. I also miss being able to limit the options for exercise types in the watch face. I have to scroll through a long list each time. Overall, pretty good product for a cheaper price.

Update: I originally gave this 5 stars, but I have to take 2 away. A few days ago, the watch all of a sudden stopped counting steps. For the price, it was perfect until it stopped working. Back to the drawing board.

reviews.title: The perfect watch

reviews.title: Keep track
reviews.text: This fitness tracker is simple to setup and in no time it'll be keeping track of your health so you have a monitor of what you do every day. It's simple to track and manage your sleep patterns or steps you take in a day. Very simple to use mobile app. The watch lasts a long time before dieing at least 2 days

reviews.title: Super impressed with this watch. Truly remarkable
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:ae47b9651b52b2c1b0485219f33680bf]] Wow. I am not impressed by much, but this watch is truly impressive for this price point.

Build: Very good build quality. The waterproof case feels solid, with responsive buttons and full color high res screen. Bracelet is soft rubber and is very comfortable. It's a very large band, so large or small wrists will fit in here.

Features: Though not perfect, the features on this watch are impressive. Measures steps, activities, heart rate, laps, sleep quality. Very very nice featuers. Plus it's waterproof!!!

Cons: Some of the interfaces are a bit clunky, and the app could use work, but it's fairly intuitive, and easy to use. I connected to my Galaxy S7 with no problems.

I recommend this watch!

reviews.title: My favorite budget smart watch
reviews.text:
I've tried a variety of budget smart watches, and this one by Dr.Viva has become my favorite. It's very comfortable, great flexible band, no issues with feeling like it's pressing into my wrist bones. The screen display is a good size without being too large, perfect for smaller wrists.

The Zeroner app has been fantastic, no problems. It accurately tracks my steps, sleep & oxygen. I like how it has a variety of exercises for me to document. The battery life is a big winner. I recharge the smart watch once a week, compared to twice a week with the other watches I've tried before. I was disappointed that I don't have the option to have my own picture as the screen display, but the app still has plenty of screens to choose from.

If you're like me and don't want to spend an exorbitant price on a smart watch, but would still like to enjoy the features of a smart watch this one would be a great option.

reviews.title: Inaccurate
reviews.text: This is a cute watch. Set up was simple. I liked the pink band and it was comfortable. The multi-lingual instructions are useless. Even worse it is inaccurate. I was at my desk and took it off. When I came back it said I logged 1800 steps.  It was sitting in the same place on the desk. Not. Back to my fit bit.

reviews.title: Very Durable and Love the Face
reviews.text:
The watch and band are more durable than I expected at this price point. The watch is very easy to install and should not take more than 5 minutes. For some reason I really like the aesthetics on the face of the watch.

I have another watch that is comparable and this watch costs 25% less. This is definitely good value. A watch like this is a game changer for people monitoring heart issues. Highly recommend.

All the measurement data and results are provided for reference only and cannot be compared with the results of professional medical devices, used to perform professional diagnosis or monitoring of any medical conditions. Due to external factors (such as arm hair, tattoos, or darker skin), there may be cases where measurement results are inaccurate or even unavailable!

reviews.title: Not a good buy
reviews.text: The 1st one I got "went crazy" and started just cycling thru the various screens on the watch.  I returned it and got a replacement because we all know that "stuff happens".  I got the replacement and it seemed to be working well until day 28, when it stopped connecting with the app at all, even though I installed and uninstalled it several times as the troubleshooting recommended.  Then it started draining my battery dry and that meant it needed to be returned.  Don't waste your money on this one unless you have LOTS of time to fiddle with it!

reviews.title: I wanted to like this product...
reviews.text:
I wanted a simple activity tracker and this looked like it was perfect. There are a number of things that worked well, battery life, simplicity, matched my needs... However, the accuracy is non existent. I was at the gym working out vigorously and my heart rate was 50 and dropping. I checked it with another app on my phone and I was approaching 140. When I am at rest it creeps into the 90's. by the way, I have entered all profile info. The steps are also way off as I tested against an app on my phone.

I tried but its not worth the effort.

title: SUUNTO 7 GPS Sports Smart Watch
features: Suunto 7: This versatile smart watch combines Suunto's sports expertise with smartwatch technology; Track exercise accurately with access to more than 70 sports modes, GPS, and wrist heart rate sensor

Make Life Easier: Compatible with Android and iOS phones, the Suunto 7 is powered by Wear OS by Google so you can see incoming calls and messages, make payments with your watch and download music to your watch to listen offline
Get Connected: Connect to your favorite sports apps and services from the Suunto app to get more from your watch; Share with your sports communities and access specific training analysis and guidance

reviews.title: Waste of money
reviews.text: Don’t waste your money

reviews.title: I like this one better than my other smart watch
reviews.text: I have a big deal designer's name brand smart watch which has a battery life of like 12 hours max and that's with disabling just about everything!  The problem with charging that (and this) smart watch is the fact that you can't just plug them in anywhere with a regular micro usb.  They have their proprietary charging "cradle" that gloms onto the back of the watch.  This one seem so far to have a higher battery life without having to shut down every app to extend the battery life.  The only thing I would wish is that if they market these to both men and women, MAKE THE WATCH FACE A BIT SMALLER!!!  I understand about seeing/reading everything on the watch, but JEEZ it is HUGE!!!  I had to fasten it further down on my wrist/arm so the watch face fit my arm.  I'm not a tiny woman nor am I a big one, so if that's a problem for an average woman, please take note and scale it back by about 3%.  Otherwise, this is a great smart watch.  Runs on Google OS which my other watch runs on and seems to do pretty much the same things.

reviews.title: My husband LOVES this watch!
reviews.text:
I got this watch for my husband who is 6'3. This watch fits his wrist really well. He has used this for over a month before the review.

CONS (very minor):
1. If you move your hand the right way it can hit the buttons on the side and start apps by accident
2. It can be sensitive when moving your hand around and turn on the clock display/run down the battery faster
3. I don't like that the charger is so specific. It would be nice to be able to have more than 1 option to charge

PROS:
1. Battery Life: Easily lasts 1.5 days before charging and it was fine. If it runs down really low it will automatically go into battery saving mode and only functions as a normal watch. It charges very quickly
2. Style: It looks nice. The face size is a good size and easy to read. The band is silicone, comfortable and holds up well to a lot of use. My husband has received many compliments on it. You can change the clock face to a few different options and customize it to you.
3. Alerts: When you get notifications of a message or call the watch vibrates. The watch has a microphone but no speakers so you cannot have a phone call through it or play music. You can type a message or even do a voice to text using the watch.
4. Calendar: The calendar function is nice and gives notifications for upcoming events. It makes it nice when he is in a meeting especially
5. Exercise: It took a while to figure out, but the exercise tracking is nice. My husband doesn't run but does like to lift weights and do other exercise. He was even able to calibrate the watch and it will count his reps for him. It also tracks heart rate, which you can set to track throughout the day. It also tracks steps.
6. GPS: The watch has a GPS and map function. You can see where you are and toggle the maps around to see routes around you. You can also set up multiple route paths ahead if you run/bike etc.
7. Durability: My husband swam with it in a saltwater pool and had no issues, it is definitely water safe. He has hit it accidentally on a few corners/desks and the glass has held up really well, no scratches. The touch sensitivity is great, easy to control but not OVERLY sensitive.

Overall it is a great watch!

reviews.title: Luxury, time and tracking for the seasoned athlete or outdoorsman!
reviews.text:
First things first: you have to be a true athlete or outdoorsman to really want to spend this kind of money on a smartwatch when there are others, but the features on this one make it exceptional. My son has truly been able to maintain his sanity after having to leave school and fly solo during this crisis. He has thrown himself into a variety of workouts to keep himself busy since he can't socialize and thinks this watch is one of the best items he owns.

The GPS features allow him to explore routes, trails, and areas he wasn't previously acquainted with (offline too!) and the health data points are accurate. There are dozens of sports modes for whatever activity he chooses for the day and the display itself is beautiful, bright and very responsive.

I wouldn't pay this much for a watch because I prefer a sedentary lifestyle (LOL) but for the athlete in your family with a long and hot summer ahead, this is the perfect graduation gift too!

reviews.title: Great watch
reviews.text:
Absolutely amazing watch. I have and iPhone and works flawlessly. Only inconvenience is the battery, it barely make it thru the day even don't using gps or other sporty functions. It would be perfect if only
Suunto fix that, otherwise is better than the Ultra for me.

title: Samsung Galaxy Watch3 2020 Smartwatch (Bluetooth + Wi-Fi + GPS) International Model (Bronze, 41mm)
description: The only smartwatch with a precise rotating bezel for navigating between apps. Larger screen, but lighter and thinner for more comfort: With its subtle rotating bezel, it's easier to see and focus on what's important on an even larger screen. The Galaxy Watch3 is thinner and smaller, and up to 15% lighter with a sophisticated look that, thanks to its reduced frame, is perfect for the wrist. Breathe easier with built-in oxygen measurement: The Galaxy Watch3 uses red LEDs and infrared rays to calculate your SpO2 levels or the amount of oxygen in your blood. Because oxygen saturation is directly related to physical activity, you can use the Galaxy Watch3 to push your routine to the next level. Enjoy a better sleep: The Galaxy Watch3 records your active sleep, deep sleep, and total sleep time to keep track and improve the quality of your sleep. Samsung partnered with the National Sleep Foundation to include educational content and provide tips on your device to help you sleep better. Do your best and then repeat it: With over 120 home workout programs to choose from, the Galaxy Watch3 helps you move your body at your own pace. Pick one from your phone and stream it on your TV. It will show you your heart rate in real-time while you exercise. Use the Ridge Sport strap for even more accurate stats. Continue your conversations: When you receive a new message, the Galaxy Watch3 shows your conversation history so you can follow them. You can also view images without needing an extra touch, making it easier to keep up while you're working out. Add some context with the smart answer: Respond to messages at all times and without missing a beat. The Galaxy Watch3's smart reply analyzes the texts and photos you receive and gives you possible answers in different categories, such as food, celebrations, selfies and much more.

reviews.title: Impressive watch with features
reviews.text: Watch has many features for fitness and heath I bought 2. Some of the screens are well thought out. Accuracy is a key benefit of this watch. Needed to use newer version of app and works with my android phone. Current version is metric and new version is to have imperial values. Vendor was responsive and resolved questions ASAP. Plan to buy new version with upgrades when available

reviews.title: lastima el software del celular
reviews.text:
Este reloj tiene varias cosas interesantes, pero lastimosamente el software del celular que lo controla tiene muchas fallas, por ejemplo cuando uno agrega una lista de contactos favoritos al reloj, esporadicamente desaparece y hay que volver a crearla. Tambien es frecuente que se desconecta el programa y el reloj, por lo que se dejan de recibir notificaciones en el reloj.

Tambien hay problemas con la personalizacion del skin, donde uno selecciona una foto de fondo, la descarga al reloj y aveces despues de unos minutos u horas desaparece y toca volver a hacer el proceso.

Tambien la aplicacion del celular esta mal diseñada, algunos botones no se ven bien y es dificil encontrar como seguir cuando se quiere configurar el reloj.

Lo bueno del reloj:
- permite recibir y hacer llamadas desde el reloj ya que trae microfono y un pequeño parlante, pero hay que aclarar que necesita al celular cerca y conectado por bluetooth.
- Reporta bien la mayoria de notificaciones que recive el celular, como llegada de SMS, emails, mensajes de whatsapp, aunque no muestra emojins.

-La bateria carga en 90minutos completamente y puede durar 3 dias sin tener que recargar.

En resumen hasta que el fabricante no mejore los problemas de software no es una buena compra este reloj.

reviews.title: Love this watch
reviews.text: Nice looking watch with accurate steps. I love the gps feature where it shows your walk on a true map on the app. Nice visual. Heart rate is very accurate as well. Haven't used music feature as much as I thought I would. Garmin makes a nice watch. Highly recommend.

reviews.title: Heart rate way off
reviews.text:
The main thing I bought this for is to monitor heart rate while hiking. Its reading is way off for me - as confirmed by a pulse ox and manually counting. The watch kept telling me my heart rate was over 100 just around the house. I have nsvt but my heart rate generally runs low. So I'm very sensitive when I have palpitations - when this watch kept saying my heart rate was over 95 I wasnt buying it. That brings into question any other readings or parameters.

I find the watch annoying and frustrating to use. The watch face does not interact very well for me. I'm constantly trying to tap it and it wont register so then the screen shuts off. Another annoying thing is it tells you to start moving around when I'm doing housework. I guess you can shut that off but I've really lost interest. Hopefully Garmin can offer some fix for the reading since its past the return date.

The positives - watch is comfortable and I don't wear jewelry. I forget its on. Its a large size so its readable if you have vision issues. I've had no problem with that.

reviews.title: Lightweight and perfect for small wrists
reviews.text: This is a great lightweight fitness tracker.  It has about a 4 day battery life and does a quick recharge.  It has a little learning curve for the activity tracking but it is right up there with my Apple Watch.

reviews.title: Exelente
reviews.text: La bateria me dura 14 dias. Pantalla se ve perfectamente bajo el sol. La app connect trabaja muy bien, en la tienda puedo descargar pantallas para el reloj. Resto de las actividades ok. Lo recomiendo

reviews.title: OK
reviews.text:
Difficult to initially connect; keeps disconnecting & 19 out of 20 attempts to reconnect fail; reading vary wildly & usually distressingly inaccurate (tested with other devices.)... I was so excited all this functionality was in one device!
I talked to tech support. It reconnected then disconnected again 24hrs later.

RETURNING
Asking for a replacement. Will re-review replacement. Hopefully this one was a defective exception.

The replacement is so much better, but I still have some (minor) complaints. I've only used the replacement 3 days and I want to give it a week at least.

Love the performance. Especially for the price! The battery will no longer hold a charge though. I am returning. Bah. I can't find it, so can't return it. I discourage this product.

reviews.title: Some Good Aspects, But Several Annoying Issues
reviews.text:
This CNPGD Smart Watch Fitness Tracker had the potential to be really great. In terms of appearance, the body of the watch combined with the band are sleek and attractive. The watch is available in 4 different color schemes (Black, Pink, Red, and Blue/Silver) and each looks aesthetically pleasing. I received the Black one and really liked the clean lines and modern design. The band fits around the wrist comfortably as well.

Before trying to turn the watch on, I decided to charge it fully overnight. I followed the instructions and photos on the instruction sheet and tried to remove the band from the watch's charging port end. Unfortunately, it was so difficult to remove that I thought I was going to damage the watch or the band at some point as I worked up a sweat trying to pry/pull it apart. I finally managed to insert the lip of a plastic spoon into a sliver of space I had gotten between the band and the watch. I kept working at it until I was finally able to get the band off. I really do not look forward to repeating this process.

Once the watch was charged, I downloaded the WearFit 2.0 App and after a couple of tries, was able to pair my phone with the watch. There are some annoying aspects about the App. In one area, I was able to specify customary units such as pounds and feet versus the default kilograms and centimeters, but in another area, I was unable to make the change. When using the watch, as I started to toggle through the functions, I realized that there was no way to toggle backwards. So if I swiped past my desired function such as the stopwatch feature, I needed to swipe past all the other functions again until I got back to the stopwatch feature. Swiping right or left or pressing the Home button does the same thing and only moves the choice of functions in one direction, forward.

I went out for a long walk and compared the watch's step and distance counter against my phone's fitness app with GPS and a very accurate pocket pedometer. After the walk, my phone fitness app, pedometer, and this watch all gave roughly the same measurement for the mileage. Unfortunately, the step count of the watch was about 2,000 steps off from the other two devices. it's unfortunate that this watch has so many annoying issues as the features such as the BP, O2, and other health trackers are quite handy. I would give this fitness tracker watch more stars if they just made some upgrades/updates to the app and maybe the watch's firmware.

reviews.title: Better than my Apple Watch
reviews.text: Got this for the kiddo. He’s 14 and Captain Destructo. This kid literally body slams trees for fun and the best and sturdiest gifts for him last about a week. Two if you’re lucky. We’re never lucky. However, it’s been a while and he wears it nonstop. Get this, it still works. He loves the band and all of the features. The battery life makes my jaw drop. I have to charge my Apple Watch at least once a day. This watch does more and has a battery life that won’t quit. I’d fight him for it but again…he body slams trees. Great purchase, guys. It’s also a smart and inexpensive way to test your kid’s level of responsibility before buying more expensive products and flushing your money.

reviews.title: Easy set up
reviews.text: Easy set up to any phone, and it offers the same features that the name brand smart watches offer for a fraction of the cost.  There are some differences with display appearances which is expected for the price, text come through like reading an old dot matrix system but it gets the job done.
